seo.title: Invoicemonk vs Wave: Which Free Invoicing Software Is Better? | 2026
seo.description: Compare Invoicemonk and Wave side by side. Both offer free invoicing, but differ in currency support, global availability, credit notes, and features.

hero.title: Invoicemonk vs Wave
hero.subtitle: Both offer free invoicing — here's how they compare on features, availability, and flexibility.

overview: Wave has been a popular choice for solo freelancers and very small businesses thanks to its completely free invoicing and accounting platform. It built a loyal following by removing the cost barrier entirely — you could send unlimited invoices and manage your books without paying a cent. But Wave's model comes with significant limitations that are often overlooked in surface-level comparisons.

The biggest limitation is geography. Since 2023, Wave is only available in the United States and Canada. If your business is based anywhere else — Nigeria, the UK, Australia, or any other country — Wave simply doesn't work for you. Even for US and Canadian businesses, Wave doesn't support multi-currency invoicing, which is increasingly essential as freelancers and small businesses work with international clients.

Invoicemonk takes a different approach. It offers a free tier that includes invoicing, expenses, and accounting — similar to Wave — but works globally with multi-currency support on every plan. Additional features like credit notes, an invoice verification portal, and tax compliance for multiple jurisdictions make Invoicemonk the more capable platform for businesses that operate internationally or plan to grow beyond a single market.

items.title: Global availability
items.desc: Wave only operates in the US and Canada. Invoicemonk works worldwide with localized tax compliance for Nigeria (FIRS), UK (HMRC), US (IRS), Canada (CRA), and Australia (ATO). This makes Invoicemonk the default choice for anyone outside North America — and a better choice for North American businesses with international clients.

items.title: Multi-currency support
items.desc: Wave invoices only in USD and CAD. Invoicemonk supports all major currencies on every plan, including the free tier. For freelancers with even one international client, this difference alone can justify the switch — billing in a client's currency accelerates payment and reduces disputes.

items.title: Credit notes and corrections
items.desc: Wave has no credit note functionality. If you need to issue a refund, correction, or adjustment, you must create workarounds that don't follow proper accounting standards. Invoicemonk has built-in credit notes that link to original invoices, maintain proper accounting treatment, and keep your books audit-ready.

items.title: Revenue model
items.desc: Wave is free but monetizes through payment processing fees (2.9% + $0.60 per credit card transaction) and payroll services. Invoicemonk's free tier is genuinely free with no transaction fees. Paid plans add features like unlimited currency accounts and team access — you choose when and if you upgrade.

invoicemonk.title: Choose Invoicemonk if you…
items: Are based outside the US or Canada
Need multi-currency invoicing for international clients
Want credit notes and invoice verification
Want team access as your business grows

competitor.title: Choose Wave if you…
items: Are US or Canada-based with only domestic clients
Only invoice in USD or CAD
Don't need credit notes or team features

migration.title: How to migrate from Wave to Invoicemonk

steps.title: Export your Wave data
steps.desc: In Wave, go to Settings → Data Export. Download your customers, invoices, and transactions as CSV files.

steps.title: Sign up for Invoicemonk
steps.desc: Create a free account at app.invoicemonk.com. Set up your business profile, logo, and tax configuration.

steps.title: Import your clients
steps.desc: Upload your customer CSV. Invoicemonk maps names, emails, and addresses automatically.

steps.title: Configure your currencies
steps.desc: If you now work with international clients, set up currency accounts for each market you serve.

steps.title: Start invoicing globally
steps.desc: Send your first invoice from Invoicemonk — in any currency, to any country.

items.question: Is Invoicemonk better than Wave?
items.answer: For businesses outside the US and Canada, Invoicemonk is the clear choice — Wave is only available in those two markets. Even within the US and Canada, Invoicemonk offers multi-currency invoicing, credit notes, and an invoice verification portal that Wave lacks. Wave's advantage is its completely free model (monetized through payment processing fees) and built-in payroll for US businesses.

items.question: Is Wave really free?
items.answer: Wave's invoicing and accounting are free, but the company monetizes through payment processing fees (2.9% + $0.60 per credit card transaction) and payroll services. Invoicemonk also has a free tier with invoicing, expenses, and accounting — but its paid plans offer more features at competitive prices without taking a percentage of your transactions.

items.question: Can I switch from Wave to Invoicemonk?
items.answer: Yes. Export your client and invoice data from Wave as CSV files and import them into Invoicemonk. The migration process typically takes 15–30 minutes. Our support team can assist with data mapping if needed.

items.question: Why did Wave stop working in my country?
items.answer: Wave restricted its service to the US and Canada in 2023. If you were using Wave from another country, you need an alternative. Invoicemonk is built for global use with tax compliance for Nigeria, UK, US, Canada, and Australia — and works anywhere in the world.

items.question: Does Wave support multi-currency invoicing?
items.answer: No. Wave only supports invoicing in USD and CAD. If you have international clients or need to bill in other currencies, Invoicemonk is the better choice with support for all major world currencies on every plan, including the free tier.

items.question: Which has better accounting features?
items.answer: Both offer double-entry accounting with chart of accounts, financial reports, and transaction categorization. Wave's accounting is completely free. Invoicemonk's accounting is included on all plans and adds multi-currency consolidation and compliance-focused reports that Wave doesn't offer.

items.question: Can Wave handle credit notes?
items.answer: No. Wave does not support credit notes or invoice corrections. If you need to issue refunds, adjustments, or corrections, you have to create manual workarounds. Invoicemonk has built-in credit note support with proper accounting treatment.

cta.title: Try the global alternative to Wave
cta.description: Free invoicing that works everywhere, not just the US and Canada.
